content:
# 🚨 MANDATORY: Team Memory System 🚨

A shared team memory stores critical gotchas, conventions, and debugging insights across sessions.

## REQUIRED: /recall BEFORE Starting Any Task

You MUST run /recall at the start of every task to check for relevant prior knowledge. Skipping this risks repeating known mistakes and wasting time on already-solved problems.

```
/recall "<topic or keyword relevant to your task>"
```

Examples:
- Working on Stripe integration? → /recall "Stripe"
- Fixing a database query? → /recall "database" or /recall "Drizzle"
- Debugging a test? → /recall "testing patterns"
- Working on team creation? → /recall "team" or /recall "createId"
- Touching waivers? → /recall "waiver"

**Do NOT skip this step.** Past sessions have stored critical gotchas that will save significant debugging time.

## REQUIRED: /remember After Discoveries

When you discover something useful — a gotcha, root cause, convention, or debugging insight — you MUST save it immediately with /remember. Do not wait until the end of the session.

```
/remember "<what you learned>" --category=gotcha|convention|debugging|architecture|workflow --priority=critical|moderate|ephemeral
```

## Summary

- **BEFORE work**: /recall to load relevant memories (MANDATORY)
- **DURING work**: /remember as soon as you discover something worth saving (MANDATORY)
- Failure to use team memory means the team loses knowledge and repeats mistakes.

## Swarm Session: 2639
Source: startup

## Open Epics
- **wodsmith-monorepo-qfnbn-mjunbceyqxp**: Review Organizer Docs for Accuracy with Screenshots
  0/6 subtasks remaining

## Hive Stats
Open: 1 | In Progress: 0 | Blocked: 0 | Closed: 40

message.content: We are consistently seeing athletes have their email set as their last name, which is Concerning and not correct. We don't want to expose private information like that on our platform. Please look for any sign-up log logic either for competitions or at the account signup level and see if we are setting the email as some type of fallback back, we need to correct this behavior. Last name should probably just be optional anyway Or we make it required and don't have this awful fallback.

addedBlocks:
## deepwiki
DeepWiki MCP provides AI-powered documentation for GitHub repositories.

Available tools:
- read_wiki_structure: Get a list of documentation topics for a repository
- read_wiki_contents: View full documentation about a repository
- ask_question: Ask any question about a repository and get an AI-powered answer
- list_available_repos: List your available repositories (private mode only)
- generate_wiki: Generate a codebase wiki for a repository — only use when explicitly requested by the user (private mode only)
- devin_knowledge_manage: Manage Devin knowledge notes and suggestions — list, search, get, create, update, delete notes, view folder structure, list/view/dismiss knowledge suggestions (private mode only)
- devin_playbook_manage: Manage Devin playbooks — list, get, create, update, delete (private mode only)
- devin_schedule_manage: Manage scheduled Devin sessions — list, get, create, update, delete (private mode only)
- devin_session_create: Create one or more child Devin sessions (private mode only)
- devin_session_interact: Manage a Devin session — get status, send messages, sleep/terminate/archive, read messages & attachments, manage tags (private mode only)
- devin_session_events: Inspect session events — list summaries, fetch full details, or search event contents (private mode only)
- devin_session_search: Search and filter Devin sessions (private mode only)
- list_integrations: List all native integrations and MCP servers with their status and settings URLs (private mode only)

## plugin:linear:linear
When passing string values to tools, send the content directly without escape sequences. For example, use real newlines in markdown content rather than literal backslash-n (\n) characters.

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- agent-browser: Browser automation CLI for AI agents. Use when the user needs to interact with websites, including navigating pages, filling forms, clicking buttons, taking screenshots, extracting data, testing web apps, or automating any browser task. Triggers include requests to "open a website", "fill out a form", "click a button", "take a screenshot", "scrape data from a page", "test this web app", "login to a site", "automate browser actions", or any task requiring programmatic web interaction.
- seo-aeo-best-practices: SEO and AEO best practices for metadata, Open Graph, sitemaps, robots.txt, hreflang, JSON-LD structured data, EEAT, and content optimized for search engines and AI answer surfaces. Use this skill when implementing page SEO, technical SEO, schema markup, international SEO, AI-overview readiness, or improving content for Google, ChatGPT, Perplexity, and similar assistants.
- self-improving-agent: A universal self-improving agent that learns from ALL skill experiences. Uses multi-memory architecture (semantic + episodic + working) to continuously evolve the codebase. Auto-triggers on skill completion/error with hooks-based self-correction.
- web-design-guidelines: Review UI code for Web Interface Guidelines compliance. Use when asked to "review my UI", "check accessibility", "audit design", "review UX", or "check my site against best practices".
- linear: Manage issues, projects & team workflows in Linear. Use when the user wants to read, create or updates tickets in Linear.
- breadboarding: Transform a workflow description into affordance tables showing UI and Code affordances with their wiring. Use to map existing systems or design new ones from shaped parts.
- repo-autopsy: Clone a GitHub repo into a local cache and perform deep analysis using full local toolchain (rg, ast-grep, git, fd, tokei, gitleaks). Use when the user wants to inspect, explore, audit, or understand an external GitHub repository — structure, dependencies, hotspots, exports, secrets, code stats, git blame, or targeted file reads. Triggers on phrases like "autopsy", "analyze this repo", "clone and explore", "what's inside owner/repo", or any request to investigate a third-party GitHub project without adding it to the current working tree.
- adr-skill: Create and maintain Architecture Decision Records (ADRs) optimized for agentic coding workflows. Use when you need to propose, write, update, accept/reject, deprecate, or supersede an ADR; bootstrap an adr folder and index; consult existing ADRs before implementing changes; or enforce ADR conventions. This skill uses Socratic questioning to capture intent before drafting, and validates output against an agent-readiness checklist.
- breadboard-reflection: Breadboard Analysis
- stripe-best-practices: Best practices for building Stripe integrations
- frontend-design: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, artifacts, posters, or applications (examples include websites, landing pages, dashboards, React components, HTML/CSS layouts, or when styling/beautifying any web UI). Generates creative, polished code and UI design that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
- vercel-react-best-practices: React and Next.js performance optimization guidelines from Vercel Engineering. This skill should be used when writing, reviewing, or refactoring React/Next.js code to ensure optimal performance patterns. Triggers on tasks involving React components, Next.js pages, data fetching, bundle optimization, or performance improvements.
- vercel-composition-patterns: React composition patterns that scale. Use when refactoring components with boolean prop proliferation, building flexible component libraries, or designing reusable APIs. Triggers on tasks involving compound components, render props, context providers, or component architecture. Includes React 19 API changes.
- shaping: Use this methodology when collaboratively shaping a solution with the user - iterating on problem definition (requirements) and solution options (shapes).
- tanstack-start-server-only: TanStack Start server-only patterns using createServerOnlyFn and cloudflare:workers env access.
Use when: accessing Cloudflare environment variables (APP_URL, STRIPE_SECRET_KEY, etc.),
creating server-only utility functions, working with Stripe or other server-only APIs,
or when you need to ensure code only runs on the server and throws if called from client.
Covers createServerOnlyFn vs createServerFn differences, env access patterns, and centralized
server-only utilities.
- documentation: Centralizes Diataxis documentation framework guidance so Claude can write, review, or improve documentation following the four distinct types (tutorials, how-to guides, reference, explanation).
- athlete-docs: Documentation guidance for competition athletes and volunteers in WODsmith. Use when writing, reviewing, or improving athlete-facing documentation including registration, scheduling, workout viewing, leaderboards, check-in, and volunteer coordination.
- ralph-mode: Autonomous AI coding loop that ships features while you sleep. Use when the user wants to run Ralph, start an autonomous coding session, work overnight, or let Claude implement multiple user stories autonomously. Triggers on "ralph", "autonomous mode", "overnight coding", "autonomous loop", or requests to implement multiple stories without supervision.
- server-fn-auth: Authentication and authorization patterns for TanStack Start server functions.
Use when: adding auth to a new server function, fixing missing authorization,
checking user permissions, protecting endpoints, or implementing organizer/volunteer
access control for competitions.
- test: Primary entry point for testing guidance. Routes to appropriate test type (unit, integration, E2E) based on what you're testing. Use when asked about "test", "testing", "write tests", "TDD", or test strategy. Embeds Testing Trophy philosophy and TDD Red-Green-Refactor as core discipline.
- claim-url-lookup: Look up claim URLs for placeholder users created via manual registration. Use when needing to find the sign-up claim link for a specific email address, debug claim token issues, or verify claim tokens exist in local KV. Triggers on "claim URL", "claim token", "claim link", "placeholder user sign-up link", or requests to find the registration invite link for an email.
- competition-organizer-docs: Generate and review documentation for competition organizers using Diataxis framework. Use when writing docs for organizer workflows like creating competitions, managing divisions, scheduling heats, handling registrations, entering results, or managing volunteers/judges.
- type-safety: Fix type assertions and improve TypeScript type safety. Use when encountering 'as unknown as' casts, manual type definitions that duplicate schema types, or unclear type errors in database queries, especially with Drizzle ORM relations. Also use when verifying types
- date-timezone: Handle date and timezone conversions correctly in forms and displays. Use when working with: Date inputs, new Date(), formatDate, toLocaleDateString, getDate(), getMonth(), getFullYear(), date parsing, UTC conversion, timezone issues, dates displaying wrong day, date-only fields in forms, YYYY-MM-DD parsing. Triggers: dates off by one day, timezone bugs, form date handling, competition dates, event dates, calendar dates.
- integration-test: Guide for writing integration tests with Vitest and Testing Library. Use when testing multi-component workflows, database interactions, React components with context providers, or full user flows. Covers the Testing Trophy philosophy (integration > unit), factory patterns for test data, MSW for network mocking, async testing patterns (waitFor, findBy), and custom render with providers. Use this for tests that cross multiple modules or layers of the application.
- permissions: Guide for working with team-based permissions and authorization in the WODsmith codebase. Use when touching TEAM_PERMISSIONS constants, hasTeamPermission/requireTeamPermission functions, adding permission checks to actions or server functions, creating features requiring authorization, or ensuring client-server permission consistency.
- unit-test: Guide for writing unit tests with Vitest. Use when writing tests for service functions, pure logic, or webhook handlers. Covers TDD Red-Green-Refactor cycle, Arrange-Act-Assert pattern, anti-patterns, pure function testing, and mocking at boundaries.
- tanstack-start-boundaries: TanStack Start server/client code boundary patterns. Use when: debugging virtual module resolution errors (tanstack-start-injected-head-scripts, tanstack-start-manifest, #tanstack-router-entry), importing from @tanstack/react-start/server, using getCookie/getRequestHeaders/getRequestUrl, adding providers to router.tsx, or when Vite fails to resolve TanStack Start imports. Covers createServerFn patterns, dynamic imports, and proper placement of server-only code.
- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills using TypeScript/Bun scripts (Windows-compatible). Use when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Claude's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations.
- drag-and-drop: Implement drag and drop using @atlaskit/pragmatic-drag-and-drop. Use when implementing sortable lists, reorderable items, kanban boards, or any drag-drop interactions. Covers draggable setup, drop targets, edge detection, drag previews, and critical state management patterns to avoid performance issues.
- my-pr-comments: Loads the pr review comments for the current branch.
- review: Centralizes persona-driven code reviews (Carmack, React core, Anders, React Router, Next.js, etc.) so Claude can pick or combine expert viewpoints when the user asks for a code review or perspective-specific critique. Use when user asks for code review, architecture feedback, or expert perspective on implementation.
- local-db: Handle local database schema changes with Drizzle and PlanetScale. Use when making schema changes to src/db/schema.ts, adding/modifying database tables or columns, or when asked about database migrations. Covers the push-based local development workflow and when to generate migrations.
- e2e-test: End-to-end testing with Playwright. Use when writing E2E tests, implementing page objects, setting up test fixtures, handling authentication in tests, adding visual regression testing, or configuring E2E tests for CI. Covers when to use E2E vs integration tests, critical path identification, page object pattern, test isolation, and CI considerations.
- team-memory: Store, search, and manage team memory from AI coding sessions. Use /remember to save observations (conventions, gotchas, debugging tips, architecture decisions, workflows). Use /recall to search memories by semantic query. Use /feedback to rate memory quality (helpful/harmful/irrelevant). Triggers on any request to save, recall, search, or rate team knowledge.
- alchemy-cloudflare: Alchemy IaC patterns for deploying TanStack Start apps to Cloudflare Workers with PlanetScale databases via Hyperdrive. Use when setting up new TanStack Start projects, configuring Alchemy deployments, working with PlanetScale/Drizzle migrations, local development with Cloudflare bindings, or deploying to custom domains.
- logging: Observability logging patterns for server functions in TanStack Start on Cloudflare Workers. Use when: adding logging to server functions, debugging request flows, tracking entity CRUD operations, correlating logs across requests, adding observability to new features, troubleshooting request tracing, working with PostHog/OpenTelemetry logs. Triggers: logInfo, logError, logWarning, withRequestContext, addRequestContextAttribute, logEntityCreated, request tracing, observability, correlation IDs.
- create-pr: Create a PR from current changes with PRD gif
- sync-diagrams: Store and synchronize digrams with codebase in ai/diagrams directory
- linear-project: Work on a Linear project with structured workflow
- test-pr: Review PR changes and provide comprehensive test coverage recommendations using unit, integration, and e2e testing skills
- fix-lint: /fix-lint
- swarm:inbox: Check swarm mail inbox for messages from other agents
- swarm:swarm: Decompose a task into parallel subtasks and coordinate execution
- swarm:handoff: Properly end a swarm session - release reservations, sync state, generate continuation prompt
- swarm:hive: Query and manage swarm tasks (cells)
- swarm:status: Check swarm coordination status - workers, messages, reservations
- swarm:swarm-coordination: Multi-agent coordination patterns for OpenCode swarm workflows. Use when work benefits from parallelization or coordination.
- swarm:always-on-guidance: Always-on rule-oriented guidance for claude-plugin agents. Use to align behavior, tool usage, and model-specific defaults (GPT-5.2-code vs Opus 4.5) while avoiding deprecated bd/cass references.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content:
Before starting work, run `lat search` with one or more queries describing the user's intent.
ALWAYS do this, even when the task seems straightforward — search results may reveal critical design details, protocols, or constraints.
Use `lat section` to read the full content of relevant matches.
Do not read files, write code, or run commands until you have searched.

Remember: `lat.md/` must stay in sync with the codebase. If you change code, update the relevant sections in `lat.md/` and run `lat check` before finishing.

content.content:
[[lat.md/registration#Registration#Core Registration Logic]] (lat.md/registration.md:32-47)

> ## Core Registration Logic
>
> The `registerForCompetition` function in `src/server/registration.ts` is the shared core for both entry points.
>
> It handles:
> - Competition and division validation (division must belong to competition's scaling group)
> - Duplicate registration prevention (per division, not per competition — multi-division is allowed)
> - Team division logic: creates a `competition_team`, adds captain, stores pending teammates as JSON
> - Team name uniqueness check (case-insensitive within competition)
> - Teammate conflict checks: not already registered, not on another team, not already invited in same division
> - Adds athlete to the `competition_event` team as a member
> - Upserts affiliate name to the affiliates table
> - Updates user sessions to include new team memberships
>
> **Important**: This function does NOT send notifications. Callers are responsible for sending confirmation emails with the appropriate payment context.
>

Base directory for this skill: /Users/zacjones/Documents/02.Areas/wodsmith/thewodapp-2/.claude/skills/team-memory

# Team Memory

Manage a shared team memory system backed by a Cloudflare Worker with semantic search.

## Commands

### /remember — Store an observation

Save a new observation to team memory.

```bash
TEAM_MEMORY_URL=https://team-memory.zacjones93.workers.dev bun run .claude/skills/team-memory/scripts/remember.ts "<observation text>" [--category=<category>] [--priority=<priority>]
```

**Categories:** `convention`, `gotcha`, `debugging`, `architecture`, `workflow`
**Priorities:** `critical`, `moderate`, `ephemeral`

Defaults: category=convention, priority=moderate

Examples:
```bash
TEAM_MEMORY_URL=https://team-memory.zacjones93.workers.dev bun run .claude/skills/team-memory/scripts/remember.ts "PlanetScale returns strings for COUNT columns — always wrap with Number()" --category=gotcha --priority=critical
TEAM_MEMORY_URL=https://team-memory.zacjones93.workers.dev bun run .claude/skills/team-memory/scripts/remember.ts "Use ULID for ID generation, not CUID2" --category=convention
```

### /recall — Search memories

Search team memory by semantic query.

```bash
TEAM_MEMORY_URL=https://team-memory.zacjones93.workers.dev bun run .claude/skills/team-memory/scripts/recall.ts "<query>" [--limit=<n>] [--category=<category>] [--priority=<priority>]
```

Default limit: 5. Results are ranked by relevance.

Examples:
```bash
TEAM_MEMORY_URL=https://team-memory.zacjones93.workers.dev bun run .claude/skills/team-memory/scripts/recall.ts "database migration patterns"
TEAM_MEMORY_URL=https://team-memory.zacjones93.workers.dev bun run .claude/skills/team-memory/scripts/recall.ts "Stripe" --category=gotcha --limit=10
```

### /feedback — Rate a memory

Provide feedback on a memory's usefulness.

```bash
TEAM_MEMORY_URL=https://team-memory.zacjones93.workers.dev bun run .claude/skills/team-memory/scripts/feedback.ts <observation-id> <signal> [--note="<reason>"]
```

**Signals:** `helpful`, `harmful`, `irrelevant`

Examples:
```bash
TEAM_MEMORY_URL=https://team-memory.zacjones93.workers.dev bun run .claude/skills/team-memory/scripts/feedback.ts 01J5K3M2N7 helpful --note="Saved me from a production bug"
TEAM_MEMORY_URL=https://team-memory.zacjones93.workers.dev bun run .claude/skills/team-memory/scripts/feedback.ts 01J5K3M2N7 irrelevant
```

## Environment

Production URL: `https://team-memory.zacjones93.workers.dev`

All commands below include the env var inline. If `TEAM_MEMORY_URL` is already set in your shell, the prefix is optional.

## Hooks

- **SessionStart**: Reminds the agent about the memory system and how to use /recall for task-relevant lookups
- **SessionEnd**: Reads transcript JSONL, extracts user/assistant messages, POSTs to `/sessions`
- **Export**: Syncs memories to MEMORY.md between `<!-- BEGIN TEAM-MEMORY -->` / `<!-- END TEAM-MEMORY -->` markers

content.content:
## Team Memory Results (7)

- **[01KJE8V0JY25KV7SM3PYSJRA66]** (architecture/critical, score: 0.08, deprecated)
  Placeholder user claim flow: When organizer creates manual registration for new athlete, a claim token is stored in KV (claim-token:{token}) with 90-day expiry. The registration email links to /sign-up?claim=TOKEN. Signup with valid claim token auto-verifies email. Signup without token for placeholder email requires email verification. Login is blocked for users with \!emailVerified (returns generic 'Invalid email or password' to avoid revealing account existence). Regular signups (no placeholder) are unaffected — still auto-verified.

- **[01KHT910WXBVFXYSV4EZF4TPFW]** (architecture/critical, score: 0.09, deprecated)
  Every user signup creates a personal team (is_personal_team=true) + owner membership. Manual user INSERTs missing these cause getUserPersonalTeamId to throw, breaking sign-in entirely.

- **[01KHVHP77VBGXKW1SQD4BVBXPD]** (architecture/critical, score: 0.09, deprecated)
  User signup creates: users row + personal team (teams row, is_personal_team=true) + team_memberships row as owner. getUserPersonalTeamId throws if personal team missing, breaking sign-in entirely.

- **[01KJKD9MWNVY8PE4D1ZBNZFQWH]** (gotcha/critical, score: 0.10, deprecated)
  `inviteUserToTeam` auto-joins existing users immediately, bypassing the invitation flow. Volunteer/registration flows that need the acceptance form must pass `forceInvitation: true` to skip the auto-join path

- **[01KJKD9NHD496S82P5FY92BP1S]** (convention/critical, score: 0.09, deprecated)
  In combined account-creation+application flows, create all data records before calling `createAndStoreSession`. If application creation fails after session creation, user ends up logged in but unable to retry (canSignUp blocks them)

- **[01KJKD9P98EQ1PMWRFBBQRQDGX]** (convention/moderate, score: 0.10, deprecated)
  Server functions that accept invitation IDs from the client (e.g. submitVolunteerAnswersFn) need an explicit existence check — otherwise anyone with a guessed ID can write records

- **[01KHKY05HFRZ9KPFC93DDJMXXZ]** (gotcha/critical, score: 0.09, deprecated)
  session_messages table needs ordinal column for message ordering—ULID alone is unreliable for same-ms bulk inserts

1830	    .optional(),
1831	})
1832	
1833	/**
1834	 * Create a manual registration from the organizer dashboard.
1835	 *
1836	 * Looks up or creates a placeholder user, calls registerForCompetition()
1837	 * with isOrganizerOverride to bypass registration window checks, sets
1838	 * the appropriate payment status, stores answers, and sends confirmation.
1839	 */
1840	// @lat: [[registration#Registration Flow#Organizer Manual Registration]]
1841	export const createManualRegistrationFn = createServerFn({ method: "POST" })
1842	  .inputValidator((data: unknown) =>
1843	    createManualRegistrationInputSchema.parse(data),
1844	  )
1845	  .handler(async ({ data: input }) => {
1846	    const session = await requireVerifiedEmail()
1847	
1848	    const db = getDb()
1849	
1850	    updateRequestContext({ userId: session.userId })
1851	    addRequestContextAttribute("competitionId", input.competitionId)
1852	    getEvlog()?.set({ action: "manual_register", registration: { competitionId: input.competitionId } })
1853	
1854	    // 1. Get competition to verify ownership and get organizing team
1855	    const competition = await db.query.competitionsTable.findFirst({
1856	      where: eq(competitionsTable.id, input.competitionId),
1857	      columns: { id: true, organizingTeamId: true },
1858	    })
1859	
1860	    if (!competition) throw new Error("Competition not found")
1861	
1862	    // 2. Require organizer permission (site admins bypass)
1863	    if (session.user?.role !== ROLES_ENUM.ADMIN) {
1864	      const team = session.teams?.find(
1865	        (t) => t.id === competition.organizingTeamId,
1866	      )
1867	      if (!team?.permissions.includes(TEAM_PERMISSIONS.MANAGE_COMPETITIONS)) {
1868	        throw new Error("Missing required permission: manage_competitions")
1869	      }
1870	    }
1871	
1872	    // 3. Look up or create athlete user
1873	    const athleteEmail = input.athleteEmail.toLowerCase()
1874	    let athleteUser = await db.query.userTable.findFirst({
1875	      where: eq(userTable.email, athleteEmail),
1876	    })
1877	
1878	    let isNewAthlete = false
1879	
1880	    if (!athleteUser) {
1881	      // Create placeholder user
1882	      isNewAthlete = true
1883	      const userId = createUserId()
1884	      await db.insert(userTable).values({
1885	        id: userId,
1886	        email: athleteEmail,
1887	        firstName: input.athleteFirstName ?? null,
1888	        lastName: input.athleteLastName ?? null,
1889	        passwordHash: null,
1890	        emailVerified: null,
1891	      })
1892	
1893	      athleteUser = await db.query.userTable.findFirst({
1894	        where: eq(userTable.id, userId),
1895	      })
1896	
1897	      if (!athleteUser) {
1898	        throw new Error("Failed to create placeholder user")
1899	      }
1900	
1901	      // Create personal team + team membership (required for getUserPersonalTeamId)
1902	      const personalTeamId = createTeamId()
1903	      const personalTeamName = `${input.athleteFirstName || "Personal"}'s Team (personal)`
1904	      const personalTeamSlug = `${
1905	        input.athleteFirstName?.toLowerCase() || "personal"
1906	      }-${userId.slice(-6)}`
1907	
1908	      await db.insert(teamTable).values({
1909	        id: personalTeamId,
1910	        name: personalTeamName,
1911	        slug: personalTeamSlug,
1912	        description:
1913	          "Personal team for individual programming track subscriptions",
1914	        isPersonalTeam: true,
1915	        personalTeamOwnerId: userId,
1916	      })
1917	
1918	      await db.insert(teamMembershipTable).values({
1919	        id: createTeamMembershipId(),
1920	        teamId: personalTeamId,
1921	        userId,
1922	        roleId: "owner",
1923	        isSystemRole: true,
1924	        joinedAt: new Date(),
1925	        isActive: true,
1926	      })
1927	
1928	      logEntityCreated({
1929	        entity: "user",
1930	        id: userId,
1931	        attributes: {
1932	          email: athleteEmail,
1933	          isPlaceholder: true,
1934	          createdByManualRegistration: true,
1935	        },
1936	      })
1937	    }
1938	
1939	    // 4. Determine final payment status
1940	    const divisionFeeCents = await getRegistrationFee(
1941	      input.competitionId,
1942	      input.divisionId,
1943	    )
1944	
1945	    const finalPaymentStatus =
1946	      divisionFeeCents === 0
1947	        ? COMMERCE_PAYMENT_STATUS.FREE
1948	        : input.paymentStatus
1949	
1950	    // 5. Register for competition (bypasses registration window)
1951	    const result = await registerForCompetition({
1952	      competitionId: input.competitionId,
1953	      userId: athleteUser.id,
1954	      divisionId: input.divisionId,
1955	      teamName: input.teamName,
1956	      teammates: input.teammates,
1957	      isOrganizerOverride: true,
1958	    })
1959	
1960	    // 6. Set payment status on registration
1961	    await db
1962	      .update(competitionRegistrationsTable)
1963	      .set({
1964	        paymentStatus: finalPaymentStatus,
1965	        paidAt: finalPaymentStatus === "PAID_OFFLINE" ? new Date() : null,
1966	      })
1967	      .where(eq(competitionRegistrationsTable.id, result.registrationId))
1968	
1969	    // 7. Store registration answers
1970	    await storeRegistrationAnswers(
1971	      result.registrationId,
1972	      athleteUser.id,
1973	      input.answers,
1974	    )
1975	
1976	    const { env } = await import("cloudflare:workers")
1977	
1978	    // 8. Generate claim token for placeholder users
1979	    let claimToken: string | undefined

1754	    // 7. Perform all writes in a transaction
1755	    let removedHeatAssignments = 0
1756	
1757	    await db.transaction(async (tx) => {
1758	      // Update registration divisionId
1759	      await tx
1760	        .update(competitionRegistrationsTable)
1761	        .set({
1762	          divisionId: input.targetDivisionId,

1764	        })
1765	        .where(eq(competitionRegistrationsTable.id, input.registrationId))
1766	
1767	      // Delete heat assignments (division-specific)

1770	        .where(
1771	          eq(
1772	            competitionHeatAssignmentsTable.registrationId,
1773	            input.registrationId,
1774	          ),
1775	        )
1776	
1777	      removedHeatAssignments = deletedHeatAssignments[0]?.affectedRows ?? 0
1778	
1779	      // Update commerce purchase divisionId if exists
1780	      if (registration.commercePurchaseId) {
1781	        await tx
1782	          .update(commercePurchaseTable)
1783	          .set({ divisionId: input.targetDivisionId })
1784	          .where(eq(commercePurchaseTable.id, registration.commercePurchaseId))
1785	      }
1786	    })
1787	
1788	    logInfo({
1789	      message: "[Registration] Division transfer completed",
1790	      attributes: {
1791	        registrationId: input.registrationId,
1792	        competitionId: input.competitionId,
1793	        toDivisionId: input.targetDivisionId,
1794	        removedHeatAssignments: String(removedHeatAssignments),
1795	      },
1796	    })
1797	
1798	    return { success: true, removedHeatAssignments }
1799	  })

content.content:
1	/**
2	 * Invite Server Functions for TanStack Start
3	 *
4	 * This file uses top-level imports for server-only modules.
5	 * Functions for handling team and volunteer invitations
6	 */
7	
8	import { createServerFn } from "@tanstack/react-start"
9	import { and, count, eq, inArray, or, sql } from "drizzle-orm"
10	import { z } from "zod"
11	import { getDb } from "@/db"
12	import {
13	  affiliatesTable,
14	  competitionRegistrationsTable,
15	  competitionsTable,
16	  INVITATION_STATUS,
17	  SYSTEM_ROLES_ENUM,
18	  scalingLevelsTable,
19	  TEAM_TYPE_ENUM,
20	  teamInvitationTable,
21	  teamMembershipTable,
22	  teamTable,
23	  userTable,
24	} from "@/db/schema"
25	import {
26	  competitionRegistrationAnswersTable,
27	  competitionRegistrationQuestionsTable,
28	  volunteerRegistrationAnswersTable,
29	} from "@/db/schemas/competitions"
30	import type {
31	  PendingInviteAnswer,
32	  PendingInviteData,
33	  PendingWaiverSignature,
34	} from "@/db/schemas/teams"
35	import type { VolunteerMembershipMetadata } from "@/db/schemas/volunteers"
36	import { VOLUNTEER_AVAILABILITY } from "@/db/schemas/volunteers"
37	import { waiverSignaturesTable, waiversTable } from "@/db/schemas/waivers"
38	import { getSessionFromCookie } from "@/utils/auth"
39	import { updateAllSessionsOfUser } from "@/utils/kv-session"
40	
41	// ============================================================================
42	// Types
43	// ============================================================================
44	
45	export interface TeammateInvite {
46	  id: string
47	  email: string
48	  expiresAt: Date | null
49	  acceptedAt: Date | null
50	  team: {
51	    id: string
52	    name: string
53	    slug: string
54	  }
55	  competition: {
56	    id: string
57	    name: string
58	    slug: string
59	  } | null
60	  division: {
61	    id: string
62	    label: string
63	  } | null
64	  captain: {
65	    id: string
66	    firstName: string | null
67	    lastName: string | null
68	    email: string | null
69	  } | null
70	}
71	
72	export interface VolunteerInvite {
73	  id: string
74	  email: string
75	  expiresAt: Date | null
76	  acceptedAt: Date | null
77	  status: string
78	  inviteSource: "direct" | "application"
79	  signupName?: string
80	  credentials?: string
81	  roleTypes: string[]
82	  team: {
83	    id: string
84	    name: string
85	  }
86	  competition: {
87	    id: string
88	    name: string
89	    slug: string
90	  } | null
91	}

93	// ============================================================================
94	// Input Schemas
95	// ============================================================================
96	
97	const getInviteByTokenSchema = z.object({
98	  token: z.string().min(1, "Token is required"),
99	})

102	  email: z.string().email("Invalid email address"),
103	})

105	const acceptTeamInvitationSchema = z.object({
106	  token: z.string().min(1, "Token is required"),
107	  answers: z
108	    .array(
109	      z.object({
110	        questionId: z.string().min(1),
111	        answer: z.string().max(5000),
112	      }),
113	    )
114	    .optional(),
115	  signatures: z
116	    .array(

119	        signedAt: z.string().datetime(),

121	      }),
122	    )

127	  token: z.string().min(1, "Token is required"),
128	  availability: z
129	    .enum([
130	      VOLUNTEER_AVAILABILITY.MORNING,
131	      VOLUNTEER_AVAILABILITY.AFTERNOON,
132	      VOLUNTEER_AVAILABILITY.ALL_DAY,
133	    ])
134	    .default(VOLUNTEER_AVAILABILITY.ALL_DAY),
135	  availabilityNotes: z.string().optional(),
136	  credentials: z.string().optional(),
137	  signupPhone: z.string().optional(),
138	  answers: z
139	    .array(
140	      z.object({
141	        questionId: z.string().min(1),
142	        answer: z.string().max(5000),
143	      }),
144	    )
145	    .optional(),
146	})
147	
148	const submitPendingInviteDataSchema = z.object({
149	  token: z.string().min(1, "Token is required"),
150	  answers: z
151	    .array(
152	      z.object({
153	        questionId: z.string().min(1),
154	        answer: z.string().max(5000),
155	      }),
156	    )
157	    .optional(),
158	  signatures: z
159	    .array(
160	      z.object({
161	        waiverId: z.string().min(1),
162	        signedAt: z.string().datetime(),
163	        signatureName: z.string().min(1),
164	      }),
165	    )
166	    .optional(),
167	})
168	
169	const transferPendingDataSchema = z.object({
170	  token: z.string().min(1, "Token is required"),
171	})
172	
173	// ============================================================================
174	// Query Functions
175	// ============================================================================
176	
177	/**
178	 * Get teammate invite by token (for invite page)
179	 * Returns invite details including competition context from competition_team metadata.
180	 */
181	export const getTeammateInviteFn = createServerFn({ method: "GET" })
182	  .inputValidator((data: unknown) => getInviteByTokenSchema.parse(data))
183	  .handler(async ({ data }): Promise<TeammateInvite | null> => {
184	    const db = getDb()
185	
186	    // Find the invitation by token
187	    const invitation = await db.query.teamInvitationTable.findFirst({
188	      where: eq(teamInvitationTable.token, data.token),
189	      with: {
190	        team: true,
191	      },
192	    })
193	
194	    if (!invitation) {
195	      return null
196	    }
197	
198	    const team = Array.isArray(invitation.team)
199	      ? invitation.team[0]
200	      : invitation.team
201	
202	    // Check if this is a competition team invite
203	    if (team?.type !== TEAM_TYPE_ENUM.COMPETITION_TEAM) {
204	      return null // Not a competition team invite
205	    }
206	
207	    // Parse competition metadata
208	    let competitionContext: {
209	      competitionId?: string
210	      divisionId?: string
211	    } = {}
212	
213	    if (team.competitionMetadata) {
214	      try {
215	        competitionContext = JSON.parse(team.competitionMetadata)
216	      } catch {
217	        // Invalid JSON, ignore
218	      }
219	    }
220	
221	    // Get competition details
222	    let competition = null
223	    let division = null
224	
225	    if (competitionContext.competitionId) {
226	      const competitionResult = await db.query.competitionsTable.findFirst({
227	        where: eq(competitionsTable.id, competitionContext.competitionId),
228	      })
229	      competition = competitionResult
230	
231	      if (competitionContext.divisionId) {
232	        division = await db.query.scalingLevelsTable.findFirst({
233	          where: eq(scalingLevelsTable.id, competitionContext.divisionId),
234	        })
235	      }
236	    }
237	
238	    // Get the captain info from the registration
239	    let captain = null
240	    if (competitionContext.competitionId) {
241	      const registration =
242	        await db.query.competitionRegistrationsTable.findFirst({
243	          where: and(
244	            eq(
245	              competitionRegistrationsTable.eventId,
246	              competitionContext.competitionId,
247	            ),
248	            eq(competitionRegistrationsTable.athleteTeamId, team.id),
249	          ),
250	          with: {
251	            captain: true,
252	          },
253	        })
254	
255	      if (registration) {
256	        const captainUser = Array.isArray(registration.captain)
257	          ? registration.captain[0]
258	          : registration.captain
259	        if (captainUser) {
260	          captain = {
261	            id: captainUser.id,
262	            firstName: captainUser.firstName,
263	            lastName: captainUser.lastName,
264	            email: captainUser.email,
265	          }
266	        }
267	      }
268	    }
269	
270	    return {
271	      id: invitation.id,
272	      email: invitation.email,
273	      expiresAt: invitation.expiresAt ? new Date(invitation.expiresAt) : null,
274	      acceptedAt: invitation.acceptedAt
275	        ? new Date(invitation.acceptedAt)
276	        : null,
277	      team: {
278	        id: team.id,
279	        name: team.name,
280	        slug: team.slug,
281	      },
282	      competition: competition
283	        ? {
284	            id: competition.id,
285	            name: competition.name,
286	            slug: competition.slug,
287	          }
288	        : null,
289	      division: division
290	        ? {
291	            id: division.id,
292	            label: division.label,
293	          }
294	        : null,
295	      captain,
296	    }
297	  })
298	
299	/**
300	 * Get volunteer invite by token (for invite page)
301	 * Returns volunteer invite details including competition context.
302	 */
303	export const getVolunteerInviteFn = createServerFn({ method: "GET" })
304	  .inputValidator((data: unknown) => getInviteByTokenSchema.parse(data))
305	  .handler(async ({ data }): Promise<VolunteerInvite | null> => {
306	    const db = getDb()
307	
308	    // Find the invitation by token
309	    const invitation = await db.query.teamInvitationTable.findFirst({
310	      where: eq(teamInvitationTable.token, data.token),
311	      with: {
312	        team: true,
313	      },
314	    })
315	
316	    if (!invitation) {
317	      return null
318	    }
319	
320	    // Check if this is a volunteer invite
321	    if (
322	      invitation.roleId !== SYSTEM_ROLES_ENUM.VOLUNTEER ||
323	      invitation.isSystemRole !== true
324	    ) {
325	      return null // Not a volunteer invite
326	    }
327	
328	    const team = Array.isArray(invitation.team)
329	      ? invitation.team[0]
330	      : invitation.team
331	
332	    if (!team) {
333	      return null
334	    }
335	
336	    // Get competition that uses this team (competition team)
337	    const competition = await db.query.competitionsTable.findFirst({
338	      where: eq(competitionsTable.competitionTeamId, team.id),
339	    })
340	
341	    // Parse volunteer metadata
342	    let volunteerMetadata: Partial<VolunteerMembershipMetadata> = {}
343	
344	    if (invitation.metadata) {
345	      try {
346	        volunteerMetadata = JSON.parse(invitation.metadata)
347	      } catch {
348	        // Invalid JSON, ignore
349	      }
350	    }
351	
352	    // Determine invite source - fallback to heuristics for legacy data
353	    const inviteSource =
354	      volunteerMetadata.inviteSource ||
355	      (invitation.invitedBy ? "direct" : "application")
356	
357	    return {
358	      id: invitation.id,
359	      email: invitation.email,
360	      expiresAt: invitation.expiresAt ? new Date(invitation.expiresAt) : null,
361	      acceptedAt: invitation.acceptedAt
362	        ? new Date(invitation.acceptedAt)
363	        : null,
364	      status: volunteerMetadata.status || "pending",
365	      inviteSource,
366	      signupName: volunteerMetadata.signupName,
367	      credentials: volunteerMetadata.credentials,
368	      roleTypes: volunteerMetadata.volunteerRoleTypes || [],
369	      team: {
370	        id: team.id,
371	        name: team.name,
372	      },
373	      competition: competition
374	        ? {
375	            id: competition.id,
376	            name: competition.name,
377	            slug: competition.slug,
378	          }
379	        : null,
380	    }
381	  })
382	
383	/**
384	 * Check if an email address already has an account
385	 */
386	export const checkEmailExistsFn = createServerFn({ method: "GET" })
387	  .inputValidator((data: unknown) => checkEmailExistsSchema.parse(data))
388	  .handler(async ({ data }): Promise<boolean> => {
389	    const db = getDb()
390	
391	    const user = await db.query.userTable.findFirst({
392	      where: eq(userTable.email, data.email.toLowerCase()),
393	      columns: { id: true },
394	    })
395	
396	    return !!user
397	  })
398	
399	/**
400	 * Get current session info for invite pages
401	 */
402	export const getSessionInfoFn = createServerFn({ method: "GET" }).handler(
403	  async () => {
404	    const session = await getSessionFromCookie()
405	    if (!session) {
406	      return null
407	    }
408	    return {
409	      userId: session.userId,
410	      email: session.user.email,
411	    }
412	  },
413	)
414	
415	// ============================================================================
416	// Mutation Functions
417	// ============================================================================
418	
419	/**
420	 * Accept a team invitation
421	 */
422	export const acceptTeamInvitationFn = createServerFn({ method: "POST" })
423	  .inputValidator((data: unknown) => acceptTeamInvitationSchema.parse(data))
424	  .handler(async ({ data }) => {
425	    const session = await getSessionFromCookie()
426	
427	    if (!session) {
428	      throw new Error("NOT_AUTHORIZED: Not authenticated")
429	    }
430	
431	    const db = getDb()
432	    const MAX_TEAMS_JOINED_PER_USER = 100
433	
434	    // Find the invitation by token
435	    const invitation = await db.query.teamInvitationTable.findFirst({
436	      where: eq(teamInvitationTable.token, data.token),
437	    })
438	
439	    if (!invitation) {
440	      throw new Error("NOT_FOUND: Invitation not found")
441	    }
442	
443	    // Check if invitation has expired
444	    if (invitation.expiresAt && new Date(invitation.expiresAt) < new Date()) {
445	      throw new Error("Invitation has expired")
446	    }
447	
448	    // Check if invitation was already accepted
449	    if (invitation.acceptedAt) {
450	      throw new Error("CONFLICT: Invitation has already been accepted")
451	    }
452	
453	    // Log email mismatch for tracking, but allow the operation to proceed
454	    // The acceptedBy field tracks who actually accepted regardless of invite email
455	    if (session.user.email?.toLowerCase() !== invitation.email?.toLowerCase()) {
456	      console.log(
457	        `Team invite email mismatch: invited=${invitation.email}, acceptedBy=${session.user.email}`,
458	      )
459	    }
460	
461	    // Check if user is already a member
462	    const existingMembership = await db.query.teamMembershipTable.findFirst({
463	      where: and(
464	        eq(teamMembershipTable.teamId, invitation.teamId),
465	        eq(teamMembershipTable.userId, session.userId),
466	      ),
467	    })
468	
469	    if (existingMembership) {
470	      // Mark invitation as accepted
471	      await db
472	        .update(teamInvitationTable)
473	        .set({
474	          acceptedAt: new Date(),
475	          acceptedBy: session.userId,
476	          updatedAt: new Date(),
477	        })
478	        .where(eq(teamInvitationTable.id, invitation.id))
479	
480	      throw new Error("CONFLICT: You are already a member of this team")
481	    }
482	
483	    // Check if user has reached their team joining limit
484	    const teamsCountResult = await db
485	      .select({ value: count() })
486	      .from(teamMembershipTable)
487	      .where(eq(teamMembershipTable.userId, session.userId))
488	
489	    const teamsJoined = teamsCountResult[0]?.value || 0
490	
491	    if (teamsJoined >= MAX_TEAMS_JOINED_PER_USER) {
492	      throw new Error(
493	        `FORBIDDEN: You have reached the limit of ${MAX_TEAMS_JOINED_PER_USER} teams you can join.`,
494	      )
495	    }
496	
497	    // Get team from invitation to check type and validate requirements
498	    const team = await db.query.teamTable.findFirst({
499	      where: eq(teamTable.id, invitation.teamId),
500	    })
501	    if (!team) {
502	      throw new Error("NOT_FOUND: Team not found")
503	    }
504	
505	    // Validate required waivers and questions before accepting
506	    // (only when there's no pending data from guest form already)
507	    if (
508	      team.type === TEAM_TYPE_ENUM.COMPETITION_TEAM &&
509	      team.competitionMetadata
510	    ) {
511	      let hasPendingData = false
512	      if (invitation.metadata) {
513	        try {
514	          const meta = JSON.parse(invitation.metadata) as Record<
515	            string,
516	            unknown
517	          >
518	          hasPendingData = !!(
519	            (Array.isArray(meta.pendingAnswers) &&
520	              meta.pendingAnswers.length > 0) ||
521	            (Array.isArray(meta.pendingSignatures) &&
522	              meta.pendingSignatures.length > 0)
523	          )
524	        } catch {
525	          // Invalid JSON, ignore
526	        }
527	      }
528	
529	      if (!hasPendingData) {
530	        try {
531	          const compMeta = JSON.parse(team.competitionMetadata) as {
532	            competitionId?: string
533	          }
534	          if (compMeta.competitionId) {
535	            // Look up competition's groupId for series-level questions
536	            const [comp] = await db
537	              .select({ groupId: competitionsTable.groupId })
538	              .from(competitionsTable)
539	              .where(eq(competitionsTable.id, compMeta.competitionId))
540	
541	            // Validate required questions (competition-specific + series-level)
542	            const questionConditions = [
543	              and(
544	                eq(
545	                  competitionRegistrationQuestionsTable.competitionId,
546	                  compMeta.competitionId,
547	                ),
548	                eq(competitionRegistrationQuestionsTable.required, true),
549	              ),
550	            ]
551	            if (comp?.groupId) {
552	              questionConditions.push(
553	                and(
554	                  eq(
555	                    competitionRegistrationQuestionsTable.groupId,
556	                    comp.groupId,
557	                  ),
558	                  eq(competitionRegistrationQuestionsTable.required, true),
559	                ),
560	              )
561	            }
562	
563	            const requiredQuestions =
564	              await db.query.competitionRegistrationQuestionsTable.findMany({
565	                where: or(...questionConditions),
566	              })
567	
568	            const teammateRequiredQuestions = requiredQuestions.filter(
569	              (q) => q.forTeammates,
570	            )
571	
572	            if (teammateRequiredQuestions.length > 0) {
573	              const answeredIds = new Set(
574	                (data.answers || []).map((a) => a.questionId),
575	              )
576	              const unanswered = teammateRequiredQuestions.filter(
577	                (q) => !answeredIds.has(q.id),
578	              )
579	              if (unanswered.length > 0) {
580	                throw new Error(
581	                  "Please answer all required questions before accepting the invitation",
582	                )
583	              }
584	              // Validate non-empty answers
585	              for (const q of teammateRequiredQuestions) {
586	                const answer = (data.answers || []).find(
587	                  (a) => a.questionId === q.id,
588	                )
589	                if (!answer || answer.answer.trim() === "") {
590	                  throw new Error(
591	                    "Please answer all required questions before accepting the invitation",
592	                  )
593	                }
594	              }
595	            }
596	
597	            // Validate required waivers
598	            const requiredWaivers = await db.query.waiversTable.findMany({
599	              where: and(
600	                eq(waiversTable.competitionId, compMeta.competitionId),
601	                eq(waiversTable.required, true),
602	              ),
603	              columns: { id: true },
604	            })
605	
606	            if (requiredWaivers.length > 0) {
607	              // Check if user already has signatures for these waivers
608	              const requiredWaiverIds = requiredWaivers.map((w) => w.id)
609	              const existingSignatures =
610	                await db.query.waiverSignaturesTable.findMany({
611	                  where: and(
612	                    inArray(waiverSignaturesTable.waiverId, requiredWaiverIds),
613	                    eq(waiverSignaturesTable.userId, session.userId),
614	                  ),
615	                  columns: { waiverId: true },
616	                })
617	              const existingSignatureWaiverIds = new Set(
618	                existingSignatures.map((s) => s.waiverId),
619	              )
620	
621	              const signedIds = new Set(
622	                (data.signatures || []).map((s) => s.waiverId),
623	              )
624	              const unsignedWaivers = requiredWaivers.filter(
625	                (w) =>
626	                  !signedIds.has(w.id) && !existingSignatureWaiverIds.has(w.id),
627	              )
628	              if (unsignedWaivers.length > 0) {
629	                throw new Error(
630	                  "Please sign all required waivers before accepting the invitation",
631	                )
632	              }
633	            }
634	          }
635	        } catch (e) {
636	          // Re-throw validation errors, ignore JSON parse errors
637	          if (e instanceof Error && !e.message.includes("JSON")) {
638	            throw e
639	          }
640	        }
641	      }
642	    }
643	
644	    // Add user to the team
645	    await db.insert(teamMembershipTable).values({
646	      teamId: invitation.teamId,
647	      userId: session.userId,
648	      roleId: invitation.roleId,
649	      isSystemRole: invitation.isSystemRole,
650	      invitedBy: invitation.invitedBy ?? undefined,
651	      invitedAt: invitation.createdAt
652	        ? new Date(invitation.createdAt)
653	        : new Date(),
654	      joinedAt: new Date(),
655	      isActive: true,
656	      metadata: invitation.metadata ?? undefined,
657	    })
658	
659	    // Mark invitation as accepted (with account)
660	    await db
661	      .update(teamInvitationTable)
662	      .set({
663	        acceptedAt: new Date(),
664	        acceptedBy: session.userId,
665	        status: INVITATION_STATUS.ACCEPTED,
666	        updatedAt: new Date(),
667	      })
668	      .where(eq(teamInvitationTable.id, invitation.id))
669	
670	    // Update the user's session to include this team
671	    await updateAllSessionsOfUser(session.userId)
672	
673	    // Handle competition_team type - also add user to competition_event team
674	    let registrationId: string | null = null
675	    let competitionId: string | null = null

906	                  string,

918	
919	            // Batch check existing signatures for all waivers at once

923	              const existingSigs =

926	                    inArray(waiverSignaturesTable.waiverId, uniqueWaiverIds),
927	                    eq(waiverSignaturesTable.userId, session.userId),

931	              existingWaiverIds = new Set(existingSigs.map((s) => s.waiverId))

937	              data.signatures.length > 0 &&

940	              const newSigs = data.signatures

951	            }

953	            // Transfer pending waiver signatures from invitation metadata
954	            if (pendingSignatures.length > 0 && registrationId) {

1057	/**
1058	 * Accept a direct volunteer invitation with additional volunteer data
1059	 */
1060	export const acceptVolunteerInviteFn = createServerFn({ method: "POST" })
1061	  .inputValidator((data: unknown) => acceptVolunteerInviteSchema.parse(data))
1062	  .handler(async ({ data }) => {
1063	    const session = await getSessionFromCookie()
1064	    if (!session) {
1065	      throw new Error("NOT_AUTHORIZED: Not authenticated")
1066	    }
1067	
1068	    const db = getDb()
1069	    const MAX_TEAMS_JOINED_PER_USER = 100
1070	
1071	    // Find the invitation by token
1072	    const invitation = await db.query.teamInvitationTable.findFirst({
1073	      where: eq(teamInvitationTable.token, data.token),
1074	    })
1075	
1076	    if (!invitation) {
1077	      throw new Error("NOT_FOUND: Invitation not found")
1078	    }
1079	
1080	    // Verify this is a volunteer invitation
1081	    if (
1082	      invitation.roleId !== SYSTEM_ROLES_ENUM.VOLUNTEER ||
1083	      invitation.isSystemRole !== true
1084	    ) {
1085	      throw new Error("This is not a volunteer invitation")
1086	    }
1087	
1088	    // Check if invitation has expired
1089	    if (invitation.expiresAt && new Date(invitation.expiresAt) < new Date()) {
1090	      throw new Error("Invitation has expired")
1091	    }
1092	
1093	    // Check if invitation was already accepted
1094	    if (invitation.acceptedAt) {
1095	      throw new Error("CONFLICT: Invitation has already been accepted")
1096	    }
1097	
1098	    // Log email mismatch for tracking, but allow the operation to proceed
1099	    // The acceptedBy field tracks who actually accepted regardless of invite email
1100	    if (session.user.email?.toLowerCase() !== invitation.email?.toLowerCase()) {
1101	      console.log(
1102	        `Volunteer invite email mismatch: invited=${invitation.email}, acceptedBy=${session.user.email}`,
1103	      )
1104	    }
1105	
1106	    // Check if user is already a member
1107	    const existingMembership = await db.query.teamMembershipTable.findFirst({
1108	      where: and(
1109	        eq(teamMembershipTable.teamId, invitation.teamId),
1110	        eq(teamMembershipTable.userId, session.userId),
1111	      ),
1112	    })
1113	
1114	    if (existingMembership) {
1115	      // Mark invitation as accepted
1116	      await db
1117	        .update(teamInvitationTable)
1118	        .set({
1119	          acceptedAt: new Date(),
1120	          acceptedBy: session.userId,
1121	          updatedAt: new Date(),
1122	        })
1123	        .where(eq(teamInvitationTable.id, invitation.id))
1124	
1125	      throw new Error(
1126	        "CONFLICT: You are already a volunteer for this competition",
1127	      )
1128	    }
1129	
1130	    // Check if user has reached their team joining limit
1131	    const teamsCountResult = await db
1132	      .select({ value: count() })
1133	      .from(teamMembershipTable)
1134	      .where(eq(teamMembershipTable.userId, session.userId))
1135	
1136	    const teamsJoined = teamsCountResult[0]?.value || 0
1137	
1138	    if (teamsJoined >= MAX_TEAMS_JOINED_PER_USER) {
1139	      throw new Error(
1140	        `FORBIDDEN: You have reached the limit of ${MAX_TEAMS_JOINED_PER_USER} teams you can join.`,
1141	      )
1142	    }
1143	
1144	    // Parse existing invitation metadata and merge with user-provided data
1145	    let existingMetadata: Record<string, unknown> = {}
1146	    if (invitation.metadata) {
1147	      try {
1148	        existingMetadata = JSON.parse(invitation.metadata)
1149	      } catch {
1150	        // Invalid JSON, ignore
1151	      }
1152	    }
1153	
1154	    // Merge user-provided volunteer data with existing metadata
1155	    const mergedMetadata = {
1156	      ...existingMetadata,
1157	      availability: data.availability,
1158	      availabilityNotes: data.availabilityNotes,
1159	      credentials: data.credentials,
1160	      signupPhone: data.signupPhone,
1161	      // Mark as approved since user is accepting a direct invite
1162	      status: "approved",
1163	    }
1164	
1165	    // Add user to the team with merged metadata
1166	    await db.insert(teamMembershipTable).values({
1167	      teamId: invitation.teamId,
1168	      userId: session.userId,
1169	      roleId: invitation.roleId,
1170	      isSystemRole: invitation.isSystemRole,
1171	      invitedBy: invitation.invitedBy ?? undefined,
1172	      invitedAt: invitation.createdAt
1173	        ? new Date(invitation.createdAt)
1174	        : new Date(),
1175	      joinedAt: new Date(),
1176	      isActive: true,
1177	      metadata: JSON.stringify(mergedMetadata),
1178	    })
1179	
1180	    // Mark invitation as accepted (with account)
1181	    await db
1182	      .update(teamInvitationTable)
1183	      .set({
1184	        acceptedAt: new Date(),
1185	        acceptedBy: session.userId,
1186	        status: INVITATION_STATUS.ACCEPTED,
1187	        updatedAt: new Date(),
1188	      })
1189	      .where(eq(teamInvitationTable.id, invitation.id))
1190	
1191	    // Save registration question answers if provided
1192	    if (data.answers && data.answers.length > 0) {
1193	      for (const { questionId, answer } of data.answers) {
1194	        await db
1195	          .insert(volunteerRegistrationAnswersTable)
1196	          .values({
1197	            questionId,
1198	            invitationId: invitation.id,
1199	            answer,
1200	          })
1201	          .onDuplicateKeyUpdate({ set: { answer } })
1202	      }
1203	    }
1204	
1205	    // Update the user's session to include this team
1206	    await updateAllSessionsOfUser(session.userId)
1207	
1208	    // Get team to return competition info
1209	    const team = await db.query.teamTable.findFirst({
1210	      where: eq(teamTable.id, invitation.teamId),
1211	    })
1212	
1213	    return {
1214	      success: true,
1215	      teamId: invitation.teamId,
1216	      teamSlug: team?.slug,
1217	    }
1218	  })

1224	/**
1225	 * Submit pending invite data (answers/signatures) without authentication.
1226	 * Stores data in invitation.metadata JSON field for later transfer.
1227	 */
1228	export const submitPendingInviteDataFn = createServerFn({ method: "POST" })
1229	  .inputValidator((data: unknown) => submitPendingInviteDataSchema.parse(data))
1230	  .handler(async ({ data }) => {
1231	    const db = getDb()
1232	
1233	    // Find the invitation by token
1234	    const invitation = await db.query.teamInvitationTable.findFirst({
1235	      where: eq(teamInvitationTable.token, data.token),
1236	    })
1237	
1238	    if (!invitation) {
1239	      throw new Error("NOT_FOUND: Invitation not found")
1240	    }
1241	
1242	    // Check if invitation has expired
1243	    if (invitation.expiresAt && new Date(invitation.expiresAt) < new Date()) {
1244	      throw new Error("Invitation has expired")
1245	    }
1246	
1247	    // Check if invitation was already accepted
1248	    if (invitation.acceptedAt) {
1249	      throw new Error("CONFLICT: Invitation has already been accepted")
1250	    }
1251	
1252	    // Parse existing metadata
1253	    let existingMetadata: Record<string, unknown> = {}
1254	    if (invitation.metadata) {
1255	      try {
1256	        existingMetadata = JSON.parse(invitation.metadata)
1257	      } catch {
1258	        // Invalid JSON, ignore
1259	      }
1260	    }
1261	
1262	    // Build pending data
1263	    const pendingData: PendingInviteData = {
1264	      submittedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
1265	    }
1266	
1267	    if (data.answers && data.answers.length > 0) {
1268	      pendingData.pendingAnswers = data.answers.map((a) => ({
1269	        questionId: a.questionId,
1270	        answer: a.answer,
1271	      }))
1272	    }
1273	
1274	    if (data.signatures && data.signatures.length > 0) {
1275	      pendingData.pendingSignatures = data.signatures.map((s) => ({
1276	        waiverId: s.waiverId,
1277	        signedAt: s.signedAt,
1278	        signatureName: s.signatureName,
1279	      }))
1280	    }
1281	
1282	    // Merge with existing metadata
1283	    const updatedMetadata = {
1284	      ...existingMetadata,
1285	      ...pendingData,
1286	    }
1287	
1288	    // Update invitation metadata and set status to accepted
1289	    // Guest has submitted their form data - this is acceptance without account
1290	    await db
1291	      .update(teamInvitationTable)
1292	      .set({
1293	        metadata: JSON.stringify(updatedMetadata),
1294	        status: INVITATION_STATUS.ACCEPTED,
1295	        updatedAt: new Date(),
1296	      })
1297	      .where(eq(teamInvitationTable.id, invitation.id))
1298	
1299	    return {
1300	      success: true,
1301	      inviteId: invitation.id,
1302	    }
1303	  })

1305	/**
1306	 * Retrieve pending invite data by token (for pre-filling forms).
1307	 */
1308	export const getPendingInviteDataFn = createServerFn({ method: "GET" })
1309	  .inputValidator((data: unknown) => getInviteByTokenSchema.parse(data))
1310	  .handler(async ({ data }): Promise<PendingInviteData | null> => {
1311	    const db = getDb()
1312	
1313	    const invitation = await db.query.teamInvitationTable.findFirst({
1314	      where: eq(teamInvitationTable.token, data.token),
1315	    })
1316	
1317	    if (!invitation || !invitation.metadata) {
1318	      return null
1319	    }
1320	
1321	    try {
1322	      const metadata = JSON.parse(invitation.metadata) as Record<
1323	        string,
1324	        unknown
1325	      >
1326	
1327	      // Extract pending data fields
1328	      const pendingData: PendingInviteData = {}
1329	
1330	      if (typeof metadata.submittedAt === "string") {
1331	        pendingData.submittedAt = metadata.submittedAt
1332	      }
1333	
1334	      if (Array.isArray(metadata.pendingAnswers)) {
1335	        pendingData.pendingAnswers =
1336	          metadata.pendingAnswers as PendingInviteAnswer[]
1337	      }
1338	
1339	      if (Array.isArray(metadata.pendingSignatures)) {
1340	        pendingData.pendingSignatures =
1341	          metadata.pendingSignatures as PendingWaiverSignature[]
1342	      }
1343	
1344	      return Object.keys(pendingData).length > 0 ? pendingData : null
1345	    } catch {
1346	      return null
1347	    }
1348	  })

1350	/**

1353	 */
1354	export const transferPendingDataToUserFn = createServerFn({ method: "POST" })
1355	  .inputValidator((data: unknown) => transferPendingDataSchema.parse(data))
1356	  .handler(async ({ data }) => {
1357	    const session = await getSessionFromCookie()
1358	
1359	    if (!session) {
1360	      throw new Error("NOT_AUTHORIZED: Not authenticated")
1361	    }
1362	
1363	    const db = getDb()
1364	
1365	    // Find the invitation by token
1366	    const invitation = await db.query.teamInvitationTable.findFirst({
1367	      where: eq(teamInvitationTable.token, data.token),
1368	      with: {
1369	        team: true,
1370	      },
1371	    })
1372	
1373	    if (!invitation) {
1374	      throw new Error("NOT_FOUND: Invitation not found")
1375	    }
1376	
1377	    // Log email mismatch for tracking, but allow the operation to proceed
1378	    // The acceptedBy field tracks who actually accepted regardless of invite email
1379	    if (session.user.email?.toLowerCase() !== invitation.email?.toLowerCase()) {
1380	      console.log(
1381	        `Transfer pending data email mismatch: invited=${invitation.email}, acceptedBy=${session.user.email}`,
1382	      )
1383	    }
1384	
1385	    // Parse metadata to get pending data
1386	    let pendingData: PendingInviteData | null = null
1387	    if (invitation.metadata) {
1388	      try {
1389	        const metadata = JSON.parse(invitation.metadata) as Record<
1390	          string,
1391	          unknown
1392	        >
1393	        pendingData = {
1394	          submittedAt:
1395	            typeof metadata.submittedAt === "string"
1396	              ? metadata.submittedAt
1397	              : undefined,
1398	          pendingAnswers: Array.isArray(metadata.pendingAnswers)
1399	            ? (metadata.pendingAnswers as PendingInviteAnswer[])
1400	            : undefined,
1401	          pendingSignatures: Array.isArray(metadata.pendingSignatures)
1402	            ? (metadata.pendingSignatures as PendingWaiverSignature[])
1403	            : undefined,
1404	        }
1405	      } catch {
1406	        // Invalid JSON, ignore
1407	      }
1408	    }
1409	
1410	    if (!pendingData) {
1411	      return { success: true, transferred: false }
1412	    }
1413	
1414	    const team = Array.isArray(invitation.team)
1415	      ? invitation.team[0]
1416	      : invitation.team
1417	
1418	    // Get competition metadata to find registrationId
1419	    let registrationId: string | null = null
1420	    if (
1421	      team?.type === TEAM_TYPE_ENUM.COMPETITION_TEAM &&
1422	      team.competitionMetadata
1423	    ) {
1424	      try {
1425	        const competitionMetadata = JSON.parse(team.competitionMetadata) as {
1426	          competitionId?: string
1427	        }
1428	        if (competitionMetadata.competitionId) {
1429	          const registration =
1430	            await db.query.competitionRegistrationsTable.findFirst({
1431	              where: and(
1432	                eq(
1433	                  competitionRegistrationsTable.eventId,
1434	                  competitionMetadata.competitionId,
1435	                ),
1436	                eq(competitionRegistrationsTable.athleteTeamId, team.id),
1437	              ),
1438	            })
1439	
1440	          if (registration) {
1441	            registrationId = registration.id
1442	          }
1443	        }
1444	      } catch {
1445	        // Invalid JSON, ignore
1446	      }
1447	    }
1448	
1449	    // Transfer pending answers
1450	    if (
1451	      pendingData.pendingAnswers &&
1452	      pendingData.pendingAnswers.length > 0 &&
1453	      registrationId
1454	    ) {
1455	      // Batch fetch existing answers
1456	      const questionIds = pendingData.pendingAnswers.map((a) => a.questionId)
1457	      const existingAnswers =
1458	        await db.query.competitionRegistrationAnswersTable.findMany({
1459	          where: and(
1460	            inArray(
1461	              competitionRegistrationAnswersTable.questionId,
1462	              questionIds,
1463	            ),
1464	            eq(
1465	              competitionRegistrationAnswersTable.registrationId,
1466	              registrationId,
1467	            ),
1468	            eq(competitionRegistrationAnswersTable.userId, session.userId),
1469	          ),
1470	          columns: { id: true, questionId: true },
1471	        })
1472	      const existingAnswerMap = new Map(
1473	        existingAnswers.map((a) => [a.questionId, a.id]),
1474	      )
1475	
1476	      const newAnswers: Array<{
1477	        questionId: string
1478	        registrationId: string
1479	        userId: string
1480	        answer: string
1481	      }> = []
1482	
1483	      for (const answerData of pendingData.pendingAnswers) {
1484	        const existingId = existingAnswerMap.get(answerData.questionId)
1485	        if (existingId) {
1486	          await db
1487	            .update(competitionRegistrationAnswersTable)
1488	            .set({
1489	              answer: answerData.answer,
1490	              updatedAt: new Date(),
1491	            })
1492	            .where(eq(competitionRegistrationAnswersTable.id, existingId))
1493	        } else {
1494	          newAnswers.push({
1495	            questionId: answerData.questionId,
1496	            registrationId,
1497	            userId: session.userId,
1498	            answer: answerData.answer,
1499	          })
1500	        }
1501	      }
1502	
1503	      // Batch insert new answers
1504	      if (newAnswers.length > 0) {
1505	        await db.insert(competitionRegistrationAnswersTable).values(newAnswers)
1506	      }
1507	    }
1508	
1509	    // Transfer pending signatures
1510	    if (
1511	      pendingData.pendingSignatures &&
1512	      pendingData.pendingSignatures.length > 0
1513	    ) {
1514	      // Batch check existing signatures
1515	      const waiverIds = pendingData.pendingSignatures.map((s) => s.waiverId)
1516	      const existingSigs = await db.query.waiverSignaturesTable.findMany({
1517	        where: and(
1518	          inArray(waiverSignaturesTable.waiverId, waiverIds),
1519	          eq(waiverSignaturesTable.userId, session.userId),
1520	        ),
1521	        columns: { waiverId: true },
1522	      })
1523	      const existingWaiverIds = new Set(existingSigs.map((s) => s.waiverId))
1524	
1525	      const newSigs = pendingData.pendingSignatures
1526	        .filter((s) => !existingWaiverIds.has(s.waiverId))
1527	        .map((signatureData) => ({
1528	          waiverId: signatureData.waiverId,
1529	          userId: session.userId,
1530	          signedAt: new Date(signatureData.signedAt),
1531	          registrationId: registrationId || undefined,
1532	        }))
1533	
1534	      if (newSigs.length > 0) {
1535	        await db.insert(waiverSignaturesTable).values(newSigs)
1536	      }
1537	    }
1538	
1539	    // Clear pending data from metadata
1540	    if (invitation.metadata) {
1541	      try {
1542	        const metadata = JSON.parse(invitation.metadata) as Record<
1543	          string,
1544	          unknown
1545	        >
1546	        delete metadata.submittedAt
1547	        delete metadata.pendingAnswers
1548	        delete metadata.pendingSignatures
1549	
1550	        await db
1551	          .update(teamInvitationTable)
1552	          .set({
1553	            metadata:
1554	              Object.keys(metadata).length > 0
1555	                ? JSON.stringify(metadata)
1556	                : null,
1557	            updatedAt: new Date(),
1558	          })
1559	          .where(eq(teamInvitationTable.id, invitation.id))
1560	      } catch {
1561	        // Invalid JSON, ignore
1562	      }
1563	    }
1564	
1565	    return {
1566	      success: true,
1567	      transferred: true,
1568	      answersCount: pendingData.pendingAnswers?.length || 0,
1569	      signaturesCount: pendingData.pendingSignatures?.length || 0,
1570	    }
1571	  })
1572

384	export const submitVolunteerSignupFn = createServerFn({ method: "POST" })
385	  .inputValidator((data: unknown) =>

388	        ...volunteerApplicationSchema.shape,

391	      .parse(data),
392	  )
393	  .handler(async ({ data }) => {
394	    if (data.website && data.website.trim() !== "") {

396	    }

400	
401	/**
402	 * Creates an account and submits a volunteer application in a single server call.
403	 * Used by the public volunteer signup form when the user is not logged in.
404	 * Avoids a bad state from two separate client-side calls where the account
405	 * could be created but the application could fail.
406	 */
407	export const createAccountAndApplyAsVolunteerFn = createServerFn({
408	  method: "POST",
409	})
410	  .inputValidator((data: unknown) =>
411	    z
412	      .object({
413	        // Account fields
414	        firstName: z.string().min(1, "First name is required"),
415	        lastName: z.string().min(1, "Last name is required"),
416	        password: z
417	          .string()
418	          .min(8, "Password must be at least 8 characters")
419	          .regex(/[A-Z]/, "Must contain an uppercase letter")
420	          .regex(/[a-z]/, "Must contain a lowercase letter")
421	          .regex(/[0-9]/, "Must contain a number"),
422	        // Volunteer application fields
423	        ...volunteerApplicationSchema.shape,
424	        website: z.string().optional(), // Honeypot
425	      })
426	      .parse(data),
427	  )
428	  .handler(async ({ data }) => {
429	    // Honeypot check
430	    if (data.website && data.website.trim() !== "") {
431	      return { success: true }
432	    }
433	
434	    const db = getDb()
435	
436	    // Check if email is disposable or already fully claimed
437	    await canSignUp({ email: data.signupEmail })
438	
439	    const existingUser = await db.query.userTable.findFirst({
440	      where: eq(userTable.email, data.signupEmail),
441	    })
442	
443	    const hashedPassword = await hashPassword({ password: data.password })
444	
445	    let userId: string
446	
447	    if (existingUser) {
448	      // Fully verified account — ask them to sign in instead
449	      if (existingUser.emailVerified && existingUser.passwordHash) {
450	        throw new Error(
451	          "An account with this email already exists. Please sign in to apply as a volunteer.",
452	        )
453	      }
454	      // Placeholder or unverified — upgrade with password and auto-verify
455	      userId = existingUser.id
456	      await db
457	        .update(userTable)
458	        .set({
459	          passwordHash: hashedPassword,
460	          firstName: data.firstName,
461	          lastName: data.lastName,
462	          emailVerified: new Date(),
463	        })
464	        .where(eq(userTable.id, existingUser.id))
465	    } else {
466	      // Brand-new user
467	      const newUserId = createUserId()
468	      const teamId = createTeamId()
469	      userId = newUserId
470	
471	      await db.insert(userTable).values({
472	        id: newUserId,
473	        email: data.signupEmail,
474	        firstName: data.firstName,
475	        lastName: data.lastName,
476	        passwordHash: hashedPassword,
477	        emailVerified: new Date(),
478	      })
479	
480	      // Create personal team
481	      await db.insert(teamTable).values({
482	        id: teamId,
483	        name: `${data.firstName}'s Team (personal)`,
484	        slug: `${data.firstName.toLowerCase()}-${newUserId.slice(-6)}`,
485	        description:
486	          "Personal team for individual programming track subscriptions",
487	        isPersonalTeam: true,
488	        personalTeamOwnerId: newUserId,
489	      })
490	
491	      await db.insert(teamMembershipTable).values({
492	        teamId,
493	        userId: newUserId,
494	        roleId: "owner",
495	        isSystemRole: true,
496	        joinedAt: new Date(),
497	        isActive: true,
498	      })
499	    }
500	
501	    // Submit the volunteer application first — if this fails, no session is
502	    // created and the user can safely retry without hitting "account exists"
503	    const { membershipId } = await createVolunteerApplication(data)
504	
505	    // Log user in only after the application is successfully persisted
506	    await createAndStoreSession(userId, "password")
507	
508	    return { success: true, membershipId }
509	  })
510

519	        email: z.string().email("Invalid email address"),
